title: Massachusetts Public Records Request — MassAbility DDS (Consolidated)
description: Ready-to-file Massachusetts Public Records Law (M.G.L. c. 66, § 10) request to MassAbility (formerly MRC) consolidating the DDS division's operational statistics with its operational/workforce/accountability records. Sections 1-4 request the unpublished Section 9F reports (FY2002-2022) and the specific open metric cells (FY2021/FY2022 receipts, dispositions, CDR, CE/MER), the FY2022 %-allowed reconciliation, the never-published homeless-claim COUNT (FY2013-2025), and the DDS operating budget FY2013-2022. Sections 5-9 request operational-defect and COVID remote-operations records (incoming-fax failure rate + NCPS template process, case-processing-system outages/helpdesk tickets, CE no-show & vendor turnaround, MER overdue counts, decision-notice mailing delays, exact DDS COVID work-from-home/telework start directive, whether initial virtual work was full-time/five days per week, later three-day hybrid/telework transition records, remote-access/VPN rollout records), workload records (weekly case-cap directives + case-weight methodology + productivity memos, per-examiner aged-case inventory), overtime authorization memos and hours, workforce records (vacancy/time-to-fill/separations, trainee pipeline, Form 30 position descriptions, grievance & reasonable-accommodation aggregates, any DDS-specific or DDS-inclusive staff climate/culture survey/report conducted by an outside firm or internally in 2017-2020), and additional special-population breakouts. Written in an individual member/steward capacity. Supersedes prr-templates/massability-prr.
geographic_coverage: Massachusetts (DDS division of MassAbility / former MRC; Boston and Worcester offices)

source.publisher: MassAbility (formerly Massachusetts Rehabilitation Commission) — Records Access Officer (request target)
source.title: Massachusetts Public Records Request — M.G.L. c. 66, § 10

update_cadence: As needed (revise FY ranges, requester name, and metric list before each filing).
notes: Confidence tier = Authoritative because this file IS the canonical, repo-maintained state public-records request template — the authoritative artifact for the request workflow, not a derived data observation. It is a parameterized request carrying no fetched dataset values; requester contact fields are filled for Wardis Vizcaino using a personal, non-@ssa.gov email address. Consolidates and supersedes the older prr-templates/massability-prr (two-letter split merged into one per user decision 2026-06-03). Does NOT reach the SSA/ODD per-state DDS budget ALLOCATION cells docs/methodology.md flags 'structurally unavailable' — those require the federal FOIA in prr-templates/ssa-foia-ma-dds. Reasoning recorded in docs/records-request-gap-analysis.md.
